name: Thai Fried Noodles (2)
recipeIngredient: Asian rice noodles, cut about 1/8 inch wide
1 lb Flat rice noodles (fresh or dried)
OR 1/4 cup Catsup or Tomato paste
8 oz Boneless pork, cut into very small pieces
2 tsp Ground dried red hot chili
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, cover the noodles in lukewarm water and let stand to soften for about 15 minutes if using fresh noodles or about 30 minutes if using dried noodles.
2. Drain and cut into 6-inch lengths, if desired. Set aside.
3. In a small bowl, combine fish sauce, vinegar, sugar, and paprika (or catsup/Tomato paste).
4. Heat a wok or saute pan over high heat. Add the oil and swirl to coat the pan.
5. Add pork or chicken, garlic, and chili; stir-fry for 1 minute.
6. Stir in the drained noodles and the reserved fish sauce mixture; stir-fry about 30 seconds.
7. Push the noodles to one side, add about a tablespoon more oil if necessary, and add the eggs; cook just until slightly set, then break them up.
8. Add shrimp and stir-fry just until they turn pink.
9. Add most of the bean sprouts, green onion, and 1/4 cup peanuts; stir-fry until sprouts and onions are crisp-tender (1 to 2 minutes).
10. Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
11. Sprinkle with chopped cilantro, remaining peanuts, and dried shrimp. Garnish with bean sprouts, cilantro sprigs, and lemon or lime wedges; serve immediately.
12. Diners squeeze lemon or lime juice to taste.
Serves 8 as a pasta course, or 4 as a main course
